title: vaccinium arenicola
intro: Vaccinium arenicola, also known as the dunes blueberry, is a species of flowering plant in the heath family (Ericaceae) that is native to the sandy dunes of the Great Lakes region in North America. The medicinal properties of Vaccinium arenicola lie in its high antioxidant content, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of consuming Vaccinium arenicola include reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Vaccinium arenicola can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, with the berries also being eaten fresh or used in jams and preserves.
